sources.label: OSI Systems IR - $19M non-intrusive inspection order (Jun 1, pre-cutoff)
StockTitan - $10M optical sub-assemblies order for semiconductor equipment OEM (Jun 3, pre-cutoff)
Simply Wall St - valuation after sharp multi-month pullback (~25% off in a month)
OSI Systems IR press releases (checked for Jun 6-9 items; none found)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the $19M NII and $10M semiconductor-optics orders (Jun 1/Jun 3) predate the cutoff and were in yesterday's hold. The latent-node thesis (under-owned imaging/sensor data with AI inference attach) is intact while the stock sits -16% YTD after a ~25% one-month drawdown, so asymmetry is modestly improved but nothing new justifies a rank move; today's +1.6% is drift.
verdicts.bull: Steady contract cadence (NII checkpoints, semiconductor optics) confirms the bottleneck position in security imaging hardware, and the sharp de-rating means the AI-inference inspection optionality is now priced near zero.
verdicts.neutral: OSIS trades as a mid-cap security/defense industrial; without a visible software or AI-attach announcement the latent-AI angle stays theoretical and the stock tracks contract flow and defense/border budgets.
verdicts.bear: The multi-month sell-off may reflect order-timing and margin concerns rather than mispricing; if FY26 Q4 guidance softens or border/port funding slips, the latent thesis has no near-term catalyst to defend the rank.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Port/border security modernization awards and any explicit AI/software attach announcement on the inspection installed base; FY2026 Q4 results in August.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Government order timing/budget slippage extending the drawdown with no AI-attach proof point.
